elastic-vis
  • elastic_vis

  • View page source

elastic_vis package

Submodules

elastic_vis.cli module

elastic_vis.cli.main() → None[source]

The main entry point for the elastic-vis command-line interface.

Parses command-line arguments, calculates elastic properties from an input stiffness matrix, and optionally generates visualization plots.

elastic_vis.core module

class elastic_vis.core.Elastic(s: str | list | ndarray)[source]

Bases: object

An elastic tensor, along with methods to access its properties.

CVoigt

The 6x6 stiffness matrix in Voigt notation.

Type:

np.ndarray

SVoigt

The 6x6 compliance matrix in Voigt notation.

Type:

np.ndarray

Smat

The 4th order compliance tensor in Cartesian coordinates.

Type:

List

LC(x: List[float] | ndarray) → float[source]

Calculates Linear Compressibility for a given direction.

Parameters:

x – A list/array [theta, phi] defining the direction.

Returns:

The linear compressibility.

LC_2(theta: float, phi: float) → float[source]

Calculates Linear Compressibility for a given theta and phi.

Poisson(x: List[float] | ndarray) → float[source]

Calculates Poisson’s Ratio for a given direction and orientation.

Parameters:

x – A list/array [theta, phi, chi] defining direction and orientation.

Returns:

The Poisson’s Ratio.

Poisson2D(x: List[float] | ndarray) → Tuple[float, float][source]

Finds min and max Poisson’s ratio for a direction [theta, phi].

Poisson3D(theta: float, phi: float) → Tuple[float, float, float, float][source]

Finds Poisson’s ratio extremes for a direction (theta, phi).

Young(x: List[float] | ndarray) → float[source]

Calculates Young’s Modulus for a given direction.

Parameters:

x – A list/array [theta, phi] defining the direction.

Returns:

The Young’s Modulus in GPa.

Young_2(theta: float, phi: float) → float[source]

Calculates Young’s Modulus for a given theta and phi.

Parameters:

  • theta – Polar angle in radians.

  • phi – Azimuthal angle in radians.

Returns:

The Young’s Modulus in GPa.

averages() → List[List[float]][source]

Calculates Voigt, Reuss, and Hill averages.

Returns:

A list containing [KV, EV, GV, nV], [KR, ER, GR, nR], [KH, EH, GH, nH] where K is Bulk modulus, E is Young’s modulus, G is Shear modulus, and n is Poisson’s ratio.

eigenvalues() → ndarray[source]

Calculates the eigenvalues of the stiffness matrix.

is2D() → bool[source]

Returns True if the material is 2D.

isCubic() → bool[source]

Checks if the material is cubic.

isOrthorhombic() → bool[source]

Checks if the material is orthorhombic.

shear(x: List[float] | ndarray) → float[source]

Calculates Shear Modulus for a given direction and orientation.

Parameters:

x – A list/array [theta, phi, chi] defining direction and orientation.

Returns:

The Shear Modulus in GPa.

shear2D(x: List[float] | ndarray) → Tuple[float, float][source]

Finds min and max shear modulus for a direction [theta, phi].

shear3D(theta: float, phi: float, guess1: float | None = None, guess2: float | None = None) → Tuple[float, float, float, float][source]

Finds min and max shear modulus for a direction (theta, phi).

class elastic_vis.core.Elastic2D(s: str | list | ndarray)[source]

Bases: object

Elastic tensor for 2D materials.

Poisson(theta: float) → float[source]

Calculates 2D Poisson’s Ratio for a given angle.

Young(theta: float) → float[source]

Calculates 2D Young’s Modulus for a given angle.

eigenvalues() → ndarray[source]

Calculates eigenvalues of the 2D stiffness matrix.

is2D() → bool[source]

Returns True.

shear(theta: float) → float[source]

Calculates 2D Shear Modulus for a given angle.

class elastic_vis.core.ElasticOrtho(arg: str | Elastic)[source]

Bases: Elastic

An elastic tensor for orthorhombic systems with optimized calculations.

LC(x: List[float] | ndarray) → float[source]

Optimized Linear Compressibility calculation for orthorhombic symmetry.

Young(x: List[float] | ndarray) → float[source]

Optimized Young’s Modulus calculation for orthorhombic symmetry.

elastic_vis.core.dirVec(theta: float, phi: float) → List[float][source]

Return a unit vector associated with angles theta and phi.

Parameters:

  • theta – The polar angle in radians.

  • phi – The azimuthal angle in radians.

Returns:

A list of three floats representing the unit vector [x, y, z].

elastic_vis.core.dirVec1(theta: float, phi: float, chi: float) → List[float][source]

Return the first unit vector associated with angles (theta, phi, chi).

Parameters:

  • theta – The polar angle in radians.

  • phi – The azimuthal angle in radians.

  • chi – The rotation angle in radians.

Returns:

A list of three floats representing the first unit vector.

elastic_vis.core.dirVec2(theta: float, phi: float, chi: float) → List[float][source]

Return the second unit vector associated with angles (theta, phi, chi).

Parameters:

  • theta – The polar angle in radians.

  • phi – The azimuthal angle in radians.

  • chi – The rotation angle in radians.

Returns:

A list of three floats representing the second unit vector.

elastic_vis.core.maximize(func: Any, dim: int) → Tuple[ndarray, float][source]

Find the global maximum of a function with 2 or 3 parameters.

Parameters:

  • func – The function to maximize.

  • dim – Dimensionality of the function (2 or 3).

Returns:

A tuple (optimal_parameters, maximum_value).

elastic_vis.core.minimize(func: Any, dim: int) → Tuple[ndarray, float][source]

Find the global minimum of a function with 2 or 3 parameters.

Parameters:

  • func – The function to minimize.

  • dim – Dimensionality of the function (2 for theta, phi; 3 for theta, phi, chi).

Returns:

A tuple (optimal_parameters, minimum_value).

elastic_vis.plotting module

elastic_vis.plotting.build_filename(prefix: str, suffix: str, output_format: str) → str[source]

Build a plot filename with the requested output format.

elastic_vis.plotting.get_style(config: Dict[str, Any] | None, key: str, default: Any) → Any[source]

Helper to retrieve a style setting from config or return a default.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_lc_2d(material: Elastic, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates 2D polar plots of Linear Compressibility.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_lc_3d(material: Elastic, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates a 3D surface plot of Linear Compressibility.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_poisson_2d(material: Elastic, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates 2D polar plots of Poisson’s Ratio.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_poisson_2d_single(material, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates a single 2D polar plot for 2D Poisson’s Ratio.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_poisson_3d(material: Elastic, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates a 3D surface plot of Poisson’s Ratio.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_shear_2d(material: Elastic, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates 2D polar plots of Shear Modulus.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_shear_2d_single(material, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates a single 2D polar plot for 2D Shear Modulus.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_shear_3d(material: Elastic, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates a 3D surface plot of Maximum Shear Modulus.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_youngs_2d(material: Elastic, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates 2D polar plots of Young’s Modulus.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_youngs_2d_single(material, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates a single 2D polar plot for 2D Young’s Modulus.

elastic_vis.plotting.plot_youngs_3d(material: Elastic, prefix: str, style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None, show: bool = False, output_format: str = 'png') → None[source]

Generates a 3D surface plot of Young’s Modulus.

elastic_vis.plotting.set_publishable_style(style_config: Dict[str, Any] | None = None)[source]

Sets a clean, publication-quality style for matplotlib plots.

Module contents

class elastic_vis.Elastic(s: str | list | ndarray)[source]

Bases: object

An elastic tensor, along with methods to access its properties.

CVoigt

The 6x6 stiffness matrix in Voigt notation.

Type:

np.ndarray

SVoigt

The 6x6 compliance matrix in Voigt notation.

Type:

np.ndarray

Smat

The 4th order compliance tensor in Cartesian coordinates.

Type:

List

LC(x: List[float] | ndarray) → float[source]

Calculates Linear Compressibility for a given direction.

Parameters:

x – A list/array [theta, phi] defining the direction.

Returns:

The linear compressibility.

LC_2(theta: float, phi: float) → float[source]

Calculates Linear Compressibility for a given theta and phi.

Poisson(x: List[float] | ndarray) → float[source]

Calculates Poisson’s Ratio for a given direction and orientation.

Parameters:

x – A list/array [theta, phi, chi] defining direction and orientation.

Returns:

The Poisson’s Ratio.

Poisson2D(x: List[float] | ndarray) → Tuple[float, float][source]

Finds min and max Poisson’s ratio for a direction [theta, phi].

Poisson3D(theta: float, phi: float) → Tuple[float, float, float, float][source]

Finds Poisson’s ratio extremes for a direction (theta, phi).

Young(x: List[float] | ndarray) → float[source]

Calculates Young’s Modulus for a given direction.

Parameters:

x – A list/array [theta, phi] defining the direction.

Returns:

The Young’s Modulus in GPa.

Young_2(theta: float, phi: float) → float[source]

Calculates Young’s Modulus for a given theta and phi.

Parameters:

  • theta – Polar angle in radians.

  • phi – Azimuthal angle in radians.

Returns:

The Young’s Modulus in GPa.

averages() → List[List[float]][source]

Calculates Voigt, Reuss, and Hill averages.

Returns:

A list containing [KV, EV, GV, nV], [KR, ER, GR, nR], [KH, EH, GH, nH] where K is Bulk modulus, E is Young’s modulus, G is Shear modulus, and n is Poisson’s ratio.

eigenvalues() → ndarray[source]

Calculates the eigenvalues of the stiffness matrix.

is2D() → bool[source]

Returns True if the material is 2D.

isCubic() → bool[source]

Checks if the material is cubic.

isOrthorhombic() → bool[source]

Checks if the material is orthorhombic.

Calculates Shear Modulus for a given direction and orientation.

Parameters:

x – A list/array [theta, phi, chi] defining direction and orientation.

Returns:

The Shear Modulus in GPa.

shear2D(x: List[float] | ndarray) → Tuple[float, float][source]

Finds min and max shear modulus for a direction [theta, phi].

Finds min and max shear modulus for a direction (theta, phi).

class elastic_vis.Elastic2D(s: str | list | ndarray)[source]

Bases: object

Elastic tensor for 2D materials.

Calculates 2D Poisson’s Ratio for a given angle.

Calculates 2D Young’s Modulus for a given angle.

eigenvalues() → ndarray[source]

Calculates eigenvalues of the 2D stiffness matrix.

is2D() → bool[source]

shear(theta: float) → float[source]

Calculates 2D Shear Modulus for a given angle.

An elastic tensor for orthorhombic systems with optimized calculations.

Generates 2D polar plots of Linear Compressibility.
